name: Lester Lanin
abstract: Nathaniel Lester Lanin (August 26, 1907 – October 27, 2004) was an American jazz and pop music bandleader. He was famous for long, smoothly arranged medleys, at a consistent rhythm and tempo, which were designed for continuous dancing.

about_quotes.speaker: Big Chielf Moore
about_quotes.evidence: So we got in the cab and went over to the Astor Hotel across in Times Square and up in the ballroom, he had his group playing there... So we played 'The Saints Come Marching In.'

about_quotes.speaker: Jonah Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Because I'd been playing with Lester Lanin, too, and Meyer Davis, and me and Urbie Green , you know, we played along with the big band

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Sherman
about_quotes.evidence: A lot of it was society, like Lester Lanin or whatever, but it was rhythmic, and we worked with a guy who, once in a while we’d get to play a jazz solo
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Sherman interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 13, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Quentin Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...black pianists had the same kind of jobs that Lester Lanin and all of those guys had then? ... [INTERVIEWEE] Oh, definitely.

about_quotes.speaker: Al Tinney
about_quotes.evidence: Especially Lester Lanin, he had a band who never had any arrangements of anything in front of him, they all played from their heads.
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Tinney interviewed by Monk Rowe, Buffalo, New York, October 14, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Jerome
about_quotes.evidence: you can play club dates, you can play these casual engagements with like Meyer Davis or Lester Lanin or whatever bands are around.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Jerome interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996
